hypothesis: If a language model is continually pre-trained on MOF-specific literature and large PubChem structural datasets, then fine-tuned MOFid-to-property prediction models will achieve lower MAE than models without such pre-training and outperform CGCNN, SOAP, and MOFormer baselines—particularly in low-data fine-tuning regimes—because domain-relevant textual and chemical pre-training imparts transferable knowledge that improves property prediction from MOFid text representations.
